cry: verb. To shed tears; to weep, especially in anger or sadness.
cue: noun. The name of the Latin script letter Q / q.
cur: noun. A contemptible or inferior dog.
cure: noun. A method, device or medication that restores good health.
curr: verb. To coo like an owl.
currency: noun. Money or other items used to facilitate transactions.
curry: noun. One of a family of dishes originating from Indian cuisine, flavored by a spiced sauce.
ecu: noun. The European Currency Unit (symbol ₠), a currency used in the European Community before the euro.
recur: verb. Of an event, situation, etc.: to appear or happen again, especially repeatedly.
rerun: noun. An act or instance of rerunning; a repetition.
rune: noun. A letter, or character, used in the written language of various ancient Germanic peoples, especially the Scandinavians and the
rye: noun. A grain used extensively in Europe for making bread, beer, and (now generally) for animal fodder.
urn: noun. A vase with a footed base.
yen: noun. The unit of Japanese currency (symbol: ¥) since 1871, divided into 100 sen.
crc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lonwolwol.
cru: noun. A vineyard or group of vineyards in France, especially one having a high reputation.
eyn: noun. Alternative form of eyen: plural of eye.
nye: noun. A brood or flock of pheasants.
runer: noun. A bard, or learned man, among the ancient Goths.
unc: adjective. Old; no longer versed in or relevant to current youth culture.
unce: noun. Early Modern spelling of ounce.
yern: adverb. eagerly, heartily, gladly, willingly, earnestly.
yue: noun. An ancient Chinese unit of volume, notionally equivalent to the space occupied by 1200 millet seeds.
